scientific_justification.what_is_being_claimed: The observed I–V characteristic exhibits a nonlinear current–voltage behavior that can be represented within a low-dimensional interpretive space defined by voltage regime, current slope, current magnitude, and structural complexity, without asserting a specific transport mechanism.

regime_axis.description: Voltage regime in which characteristic current behavior is observed

slope_axis.description: Rate of current change with respect to voltage

structure_axis.description: Global structural complexity of the I–V curve

l1_observed_coordinates.evidence: Current increases slowly from 1.0e-12 A at 0.0 V to 4.5e-12 A at 1.5 V.
Current increases sharply from 1.0e-9 A at 1.6 V to 1.0e-5 A at 2.0 V.
Current increases gradually from 1.0e-5 A at 2.0 V to 9.6e-3 A at 5.0 V.

mechanism_proposals.interpretation_note: A p–n junction transport mechanism may be proposed if finite low-field current and smooth exponential increase dominate the observed coordinate space.
A Fowler–Nordheim tunneling mechanism may be proposed if low-field current suppression and abrupt turn-on behavior dominate the observed coordinate space.
A thermionic emission mechanism may be proposed if current magnitude and slope indicate thermally activated carrier transport.

scientific_justification.important_note: This scientific justification defines a coordinate-based interpretive framework in which transport mechanisms are conditionally proposed based on observed I–V behavior projected onto a four-axis space. No mechanism is asserted without satisfaction of the corresponding coordinate conditions.
